mappings.rationaleAI: Core match addressing all three laws of thermodynamics, entropy, free energy, internal energy, absolute zero, and temperature -- directly relevant to A.2 through A.6 and A.9.
Explores entropy, the Carnot cycle, caloric theory, and energy transitions, directly relevant to A.3 (second law), A.4.c.ii (theories of heat including caloric), and A.5.a (Carnot cycle).
Examines equations of state, phase equilibria, states of matter, and thermodynamic properties -- relevant to A.5 (thermodynamic relations), B (gas laws), C (liquid properties), and D (solutions).
Addresses energy, entropy, and physical chemistry fundamentals relevant to chemical equilibrium (A.5.g) and thermodynamic aspects of solutions and phase changes.
Directly addresses fluid dynamics, hydrodynamics, and flow behaviour -- core match for H (transport phenomena, hydrodynamic aspects) and A.5.f (bulk flow).
Explores the fundamental nature and states of matter including atomic structure and mass, providing broad context for B (gaseous state), C (liquid state), and F (plasma state).

Examines Boltzmann statistics, liquids, and soft condensed matter including colloids and polymers in solution -- relevant to E.4 (colloids), D (solutions of polymers), and H (transport in suspensions and polymers).
Explores the liquid state properties and molecular structure of water -- relevant to C (liquid state behaviour and properties) and D (water as solvent in solutions).
Directly addresses superconductivity and related low-temperature phenomena -- core match for G.1.b (superconductivity and superfluidity at very low temperatures).
Explores phase transitions, superconductivity, and states of matter -- relevant to G.1 (properties at low temperatures), A.5.c (phase changes), and G.3 (effects of high pressure).
Examines hydrogen bonding, molecular recognition, and intermolecular forces -- relevant to D.3 (solvent-solute interactions, hydrogen bonding) and C.2 (molecular structure of liquids).
Explores acoustics, frequency, resonance, and sound propagation -- directly relevant to C.3.b (acoustic properties and propagation of sound waves in liquids).
Addresses nuclear fusion, thermal processes, and tritium -- relevant to F.4 (formation of plasmas), F.8 (plasma applications for power production), and G.2 (matter at high temperatures).
Solar physics, magnetic fields, and dynamic forces in the Sun are directly relevant to F.9 (plasmas in the Sun and stars) and F.5 (plasma behaviour in magnetic fields).
Stellar temperatures, nuclear fusion, and stellar evolution are relevant to F.9 (plasmas in the Sun and stars) and G.2 (properties of matter at high temperatures).
Provides broad physics context for matter, force, and fundamental concepts that underpin thermodynamics and the states of matter discussed throughout this section.
Entropy and Maxwell's demon -- the famous thought experiment central to understanding the second law of thermodynamics (A.3) and statistical thermodynamics (A.9).
Traces Robert Boyle and the historical development of physical chemistry -- relevant to A.4.c.ii (theories of heat including phlogiston and caloric) and B.2.a (simple gas laws, Boyle's law).
